title: Daily Briefing — Apr 06
headline: Extreme fear lingers, but the canary (XLF) just chirped up — respect the flows.
body: Right, big picture: The market's still trading with a VIX at 25 and Fear & Greed deep in Extreme Fear at 19, but we’re seeing volatility contract (VIX below its 20-day). SPY’s limping up by +0.1% but remains stuck below its 20 and 50 MA — bears have the technical edge for now unless we see a decisive reclaim above $658.09.

Sector rotation is noisy: Energy (XLE) and Materials (XLB) are ‘weakening’, while Tech (XLK), Discretionary (XLY), and Financials (XLF) are flipping to 'improving' but still risk-on with caution. XLF at $49.53 nudges above the 20 MA ($49.14) — if financials firm up here, the 'canary in the coal mine' sings risk is stabilizing. Utilities (XLU), Real Estate (XLRE) and Staples (XLP) are starting to roll off leadership.

Contrarian setup: With retail clearly scared (Fear & Greed 19, VIX stretched) and loads of bear biases lingering in leadership (XLK, QQQ, META, GOOGL all below MAs), keep cool heads — reversals often spark from extremes like this. Per rulebook: Extreme Fear is opportunity, not panic. But we wait for the 20 MA reclaim for confirmation — patience, react, don’t predict.
callout_body:
SPY: $658.09 (SMA20), $674.97 (SMA50) — reclaim confirms risk-on
XLF: $49.14 (SMA20) — above is constructive, financials critical
XLE: $58.83 (SMA20) — hold or fade
VIX: Watch for break back under 25 to mark further risk-on
Fear & Greed: <25 = contrarian watch for bottoming flows
action_items: Do NOT chase breakdowns here — abundance mindset, wait for leadership (SPY/XLK/QQQ) to clear the 20 MA before sizing up
Watch for XLF (financials) confirmation above $49.14 as the macro canary; a sustained move higher is supportive for bulls
closing_quote: If everyone’s scared, I’m shopping — but only above the 20 MA. Less trades, more wins.
